IterationTransforma
Transform some common for-in loop patterns into efficient C loops:
    - for-in-dict loop becomes a while loop calling PyDict_Next()
    - for-in-enumerate is replaced by an external counter variable
    - for-in-range loop becomes a plain C for loop
    c
operator
operand1
operand2r
value
stats
	condition
body)

SwitchTransformz
    This transformation tries to turn long if statements into C switch statements.
    The requirement is that every clause be an (or of) var == value, where the var
    is common among all clauses and both var and value are ints.
    )
NNNc

EarlyReplaceBuiltinCallsa
Optimize some common calls to builtin types *before* the type
    analysis phase and *after* the declarations analysis phase.
    This transform cannot make use of any argument types, but it can
    restructure the tree in a way that the type analysis phase can
    respond to.
    Introducing C function calls here may not be a good idea.  Move
    them to the OptimizeBuiltinCalls transform instead, which runs
    after type analysis.
    c
_function_is_builtin_name
_dispatch_to_handlerr
visit_SimpleCallNode
z-EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls.visit_SimpleCallNodec
positional_argsr 
keyword_args)
visit_GeneralCallNode
z.EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls.visit_GeneralCallNodec
NFT)
lookupr
builtin_scopeZ
lookup_here)
envr
z2EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls._function_is_builtin_nameNc

kwargsZ

        r
c_long_typer
unicode_valuer
_handle_simple_function_ordQ
z4EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls._handle_simple_function_ordc
Transform
        _result = all(p(x) for L in LL for x in L)
        into
        for L in LL:
            for x in L:
                if not p(x):
                    return False
        else:
            return True
        F
_transform_any_all
_handle_simple_function_allj
z4EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls._handle_simple_function_allc
Transform
        _result = any(p(x) for L in LL for x in L)
        into
        for L in LL:
            for x in L:
                if p(x):
                    return True
        else:
            return False
        Tr
_handle_simple_function_anyz
z4EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls._handle_simple_function_anyc

z+EarlyReplaceBuiltinCalls._transform_any_all
Transform sorted(genexpr) and sorted([listcomp]) into
        [listcomp].sort().  CPython just reads the iterable into a
        list and calls .sort() on it.  Expanding the iterable in a
        listcomp is still faster and the result can be sorted in
        place.
        r
sorted
comprehension_type
PySequence_ListTr
may_hold_nonerx
needs_none_check

OptimizeBuiltinCallsa<
Optimize some common methods calls and instantiation patterns
    for builtin types *after* the type analysis phase.
    Running after type analysis, this transform can only perform
    function replacements that do not alter the function return type
    in a way that was not anticipated by the type analysis.
    c
z:Flatten redundant type checks after tree changes.
        )
Z	reanalyser;
visit_PyTypeTestNode
z)OptimizeBuiltinCalls.visit_PyTypeTestNodec
        Drop redundant type casts.
        )
_visit_TypecastNode
z(OptimizeBuiltinCalls._visit_TypecastNodec
j	r`|
        Drop dead code and useless coercions.
        N)

z<Drop redundant conversion nodes after tree changes.
        )

z3Drop redundant conversion nodes after tree changes.r
z=float() argument must be a string or a number, not 'NoneType'Z

visit_CoerceToPyTypeNode$
z-OptimizeBuiltinCalls.visit_CoerceToPyTypeNodec
Drop redundant conversion nodes after tree changes.
        Also, optimise away calls to Python's builtin int() and
        float() if the result is going to be coerced back into a C
        type anyway.
        )
is_floatr
is_unicode_charr

z%OptimizeBuiltinCalls._handle_functionc
}	|	d
        Try to inject C-API calls for unbound method calls to builtin types.
        While the method declarations in Builtin.py already handle this, we
        can additionally resolve bound and unbound methods here that were
        assigned to variables ahead of time.
        )

_handle_method
z#OptimizeBuiltinCalls._handle_methodc
        Try to inject an unbound method call for a call to a method of a known builtin type.
        This enables caching the underlying C function of the method at runtime.
        r
)	rH
CachedBuiltinMethodCallNode)
z:OptimizeBuiltinCalls._optimise_generic_builtin_method_callr-

_handle_simple_function_unicode
z4OptimizeBuiltinCalls._handle_simple_function_unicodec
Simplify or avoid plain string formatting of a unicode value.
        This seems misplaced here, but plain unicode formatting is essentially
        a call to the unicode() builtin, which is optimised right above.
        r
N)	r:

Z$d9d:

Z*e+j,j-Z.
ConstantFoldingaF
Calculate the result of constant expressions to store it in
    ``expr_node.constant_result``, and replace trivial cases by their
    constant result.
    General rules:
    - We calculate float constants to make them available to the
      compiler, but we do not aggregate them into a single literal
      node to prevent any loss of precision.
    - We recursively calculate constants from non-literal nodes to
      make them available to the compiler, but we only aggregate
      literal nodes at each step.  Non-literal nodes are never merged
      into a single node.
    Fc
        The reevaluate argument specifies whether constant values that were
        previously computed should be recomputed.
        N)
superr
reevaluate)
	__class__r
ConstantFolding.__init__c
qHq4t	|
file)
constant_value_not_setr
calculate_constant_result
ValueError
	TypeErrorrO
IndexErrorr^
ArithmeticErrorr
	traceback
	print_exc
stdout)
childrenZ
child_result
childr"
_calculate_const
z ConstantFolding._calculate_constc
NODE_TYPE_ORDER
maxrT
nodesr

z"ConstantFolding._widest_node_classc

z(ConstantFolding.visit_FormattedValueNodec
        Clean up after the parser by discarding empty Unicode strings and merging
        substring sequences.  Empty or single-value join lists are not uncommon
        because f-string format specs are always parsed into JoinedStrNodes.
        c

z"ConstantFolding.visit_ExprStatNode)
F)0rB
__classcell__r
FinalOptimizePhasea!
    This visitor handles several commuting optimizations, and is run
    just before the C code generation phase.
    The optimizations currently implemented in this class are:
        - eliminate None assignment and refcounting for first assignment.
        - isinstance -> typecheck for cdef types
        - eliminate checks for None and/or types that became redundant after tree changes
        - eliminate useless string formatting steps
        - replace Python function calls that look like method calls by a faster PyMethodCallNode
    Fc
zaAvoid redundant initialisation of local variables before their
        first assignment.
        T)
lhs_of_first_assignment)
visit_SingleAssignmentNodev
z-FinalOptimizePhase.visit_SingleAssignmentNodec
j'j)|
j'j)|
j+j,|
        Replace generic calls to isinstance(x, type) by a more efficient type check.
        Replace likely Python method calls by a specialised PyMethodCallNode.
        r 
PyObject_TypeCheckrM
z&optimize.unpack_method_calls_in_pyinitz
optimize.unpack_method_callsTFc
assignment
non_method_nodesr
z:FinalOptimizePhase.visit_SimpleCallNode.<locals>.<genexpr>)
)-r:
is_cfunctionr
in_looprx
is_module_scoper 
Z	ClassNodeZ
Py3ClassNoder
PyMethodCallNodeZ	from_node)
may_be_a_methodr
z'FinalOptimizePhase.visit_SimpleCallNodec
visit_NumPyMethodCallNode
z,FinalOptimizePhase.visit_NumPyMethodCallNodec
Remove tests for alternatively allowed None values from
        type tests when we know that the argument cannot be None
        anyway.
        T)
notnoner!
z'FinalOptimizePhase.visit_PyTypeTestNodec
z_Remove None checks from expressions that definitely do not

visit_NoneCheckNode
z&FinalOptimizePhase.visit_NoneCheckNodec
zeRemember when we enter a loop as some expensive optimisations might still be worth it there.

old_valr
visit_LoopNode
z!FinalOptimizePhase.visit_LoopNodeN)
ConsolidateOverflowChecka5
    This class facilitates the sharing of overflow checking among all nodes
    of a nested arithmetic expression.  For example, given the expression
    a*b + c, where a, b, and x are all possibly overflowing ints, the entire
    sequence will be evaluated and the overflow bit checked only at the end.
    Nc
overflow_bit_noder:
savedr
z#ConsolidateOverflowCheck.visit_Nodec
overflow_checkZ
